question.text: In the price-time priority order matching system, between two buy orders at the same price, which order gets executed first?

answer.text: The order that was entered earlier (time priority)
The order from the higher-net-worth client

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When orders are at the same price, the earlier order (entered first) gets priority — this is the time component of price-time priority. **The Trap:** Volume doesn't determine priority. Client net worth is irrelevant. Selection is deterministic, not random. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers order matching rules. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Time priority rewards early order placement — in competitive markets, even milliseconds matter. This is why HFT firms invest heavily in low-latency infrastructure.
